content.full_text:
October 22, 1976


Mr. Carl G. Schulz
Committee for Flintridge Status Quo
465 East Union Street, Suite 102
Pasadena, California 91101

Dear Mr. Schulz:

Thank you for your letter of October 12, concerning your organization's filing obligations under the Political Reform Act.

The law states:

"Committee means any person or combination of persons who directly or indirectly receives contributions or makes expenditures or contributions for the purpose of influencing or attempting to influence the action of the voters for or against the nomination or election of one or more candidates, or the passage or defeat of any measure, including any committee or subordinate of a political party, whether national, state or local if:

(a) Contributions received total five hundred dollars ($500) or more in a calendar year;

(b) expenditures and contributions made, other than contributions described in subsection (c), total five hundred dollars ($500) or more in a calendar year; or

(c) contributions of cash, checks and other cash equivalents paid directly to candidates and committees total five thousand dollars ($5,000) or more in a calendar year. Persons or combinations of persons who are covered by this subsection but not by subsections (a) or (b) are deemed to be committees only for purposes of Chapter 4 of this title."

Please take a look at a, b and c of the above definition of a committee and read the examples I have circled on the attached

Mr. Carl G. Schulz                          -2-                          October 22, 1976


regulations. If it appears that your group could be a committee
which has to file campaign statements as outlined in this
information provided, I have included a packet of fact sheets
and forms to assist you.

If you are still confused, please feel free to call our Los Angeles
office at 620-5196 or the Los Angeles County Registrar-Recorder's
office at 974-6791. You may, of course, write again to our
Sacramento office if you prefer.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        REGINA SICILIANO-KUTCHINS
                                        Technical Assistance Program Coordinator


RSK:mak
Enclosures
  bcc: Jeanne

COMMITTEE FOR FLINTRIDGE STATUS QUO
465 East Union Street, Suite 102, Pasadena, California 91101 • 792-1744

October 12, 1976


Fair Political Practices Commission,
State of California,
1100 K Street,
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen:

For the first time information came to me on October 8th

that our little group of property owners opposing the incor-

poration of Flintridge, might be required under the law to

report its activities to the Fair Political Practices Commission.

Will you kindly advise if this information is correct in view

of the following:

We are not supporting any candidate for political
office;

We are not engaged in any political activity except
to defend our property rights;

We are a strictly informal, unincorporated group,
having no officers except that I keep an accurate
record of receipts and disbursements;

There are no loans involved and there will be none;

Cash in bank on October 8th was $472.51.

I must confess I am uninformed and inexperienced in these

matters and shall greatly appreciate your advice.

Very truly yours,

Carl G. Schulz
